The linting and other actions are failing in this branch. What you have to do is just orchestrate the agents like Gemini CLI and Codex from OpenAI, which are already installed in my system. If you want to make some work get done and you know what to do, just orchestrate those like OpenAI Codex and Gemini CLI. They'll do.

First of all you have to fix it and if it is big work you can delegate those tasks to them and also update these points in my global cloud.md where you can have other agents available with you like OpenAI Codex and Google's Gemini CLI.

I will publish it as a proper skill or a type of agent which others can use. Which can be used inside Claude code, Gemini CLI, or OpenAI codecs. For this you don't need any API key. For the AI layer you don't need any API. You just need the API for the Apollo if you want the emails to be fetched from Apollo API.

I guess B would be a very good thing to do and B is actually good. In B we can have our code also. In skill.md we can write some commands and code and in a folder we can have some code to execute so option B would be very great.

Want this skill to be very efficient. It doesn't need to write every time the code itself so the code and the scripts and all the things should be there already in some type of code to execute the good code to execute, which would be the Python. Sometimes it needs the environment thing but still it is very easy to execute so I would prefer not to go with the type script, just go with the Python because it is a scripting language. Yes it would be itself the orchestrator to execute the scripts. It should be very optimized not to write the same code every time I want to outreach for different tasks.

https://github.com/unclecode/crawl4ai.git I want you to use this crawl for AI. It is the best web crawler you can get out of the open source. If no Apollo keys are provided, the user can go with web search with this and skip the SerpAPI. Not using SerpAPI for now as it will give a lot of tokens to the AI. Yes it should follow all the Apollo API documentation with 2026 documentation to enrich the emails.

For sending I will use the SMTP method. This will require the user's app password from Google to manage your account but the user should have enabled two-factor authentication so I would go with SMTP with Google.

Okay the CSV can be the source of truth for now. We can have a visual, like something like Excel SX, to make it more visually appealing for the user to know which emails are being sent or not. So after sending emails it will make the rows green or some type of tracking. Is there some type of chips with some states to track it?
Yes this skill will be. I will upload this and click Get up so any user can download it and just give it to Claude and it will set it up for that. Yes it should be there in the Claude skills repo.
